message:
🌱 Add backstage: 🐛 Bug Report: `publish:github:pull-request` fails with mission (#911)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "backstage-17188-bug-report-publish-github-pull-request-fails-with-httperror-you-",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "backstage: 🐛 Bug Report: `publish:github:pull-request` fails with `HttpError: You have exceeded a secondary rate limit.`",
+    "description": "🐛 Bug Report: `publish:github:pull-request` fails with `HttpError: You have exceeded a secondary rate limit.`. This issue affects 37+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the publish:github:pull-request rate limit error",
+        "description": "Check your Backstage application logs for the secondary rate limit error:\n```bash\n# If running Backstage locally via npm\nnpm run start 2>&1 | grep -i 'rate limit\\|HttpError'\n\n# If running Backstage via Docker\ndocker logs backstage --tail=100 | grep -i 'rate limit\\|HttpError'\n```\nLook for `HttpError: You have exceeded a secondary rate limit` in scaffolder task logs. This occurs when the `publish:github:pull-request` action sends too many files in a single commit via the GitHub API."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review template configuration for large file sets",
+        "description": "The root cause is that the scaffolder sends every file in the workspace as part of the pull request, even unchanged files. This triggers GitHub's secondary rate limit when repositories are large.\n\nReview your scaffolder template to identify which `fetch:template` or `fetch:plain` steps pull in large directory trees. Check `app-config.yaml` for scaffolder configuration:\n```bash\ncat app-config.yaml | grep -A 20 scaffolder\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the skeleton/flesh pattern workaround",
+        "description": "Use the \"skeleton/flesh\" pattern to only send files that have changed. This involves fetching the existing repo content, then overlaying your template changes on top:\n\n```yaml\nsteps:\n  - id: fetch-repo\n    name: Pull latest repo content\n    action: fetch:plain\n    input:\n      url: https://github.com/${{ (parameters.repoUrl | parseRepoUrl).owner }}/${{ (parameters.repoUrl | parseRepoUrl).repo }}.git\n      targetPath: flesh/repo\n\n  - id: fetch-skeleton\n    name: Fetch template skeleton (changed files only)\n    action: fetch:template\n    input:\n      url: ./skeleton\n      targetPath: flesh/repo\n      values:\n        name: ${{ parameters.name }}\n\n  - id: publish-pr\n    name: Create pull request with only changed files\n    action: publish:github:pull-request\n    input:\n      repoUrl: ${{ parameters.repoUrl }}\n      sourcePath: flesh/repo\n      title: ${{ parameters.prTitle }}\n```\n\nThis ensures only modified files are included in the PR, avoiding the rate limit. See the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm the rate limit error is resolved",
+        "description": "Re-run the scaffolder template and verify the pull request is created successfully:\n1. Open the Backstage UI and navigate to the Software Templates page\n2. Run the template that previously failed\n3. Check the scaffolder task logs for successful PR creation\n4. Verify the PR appears in your GitHub repository with only the expected changed files"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Our current workaround is to only send the files that have changed.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+      "backstage",

+  },
